entries.d: The big bright thing in the sky that gives us light and heat

entries.d: The big water around land, salty and very wide

entries.d: The sandy place next to the water where you can sit
entries.s: We built a sandcastle on the sandy _____ next to the blue sea last summer.

entries.d: A place with houses, shops, and streets where people live

entries.d: Water that moves through the land from the mountains to the sea

entries.d: A very small town with only a few houses
entries.s: My grandma lives in a small _____ in the mountains.

entries.d: A big open piece of land where food grows or animals eat
entries.s: The cows were eating grass in the large open _____ next to the farm.

entries.d: A high part of the land, but smaller than a mountain
entries.s: We ran up the small grassy _____ and looked at the view of the town below.

entries.d: The low land between two mountains or hills
entries.s: A small river runs through the _____ between the two mountains.

entries.d: A big area of water with land all around it
entries.s: We went swimming in the _____ in the mountains. The fresh water was cold!

entries.d: A big area with many trees close together
entries.s: We walked through the dark _____ full of tall pine trees and heard many birds.

entries.d: Very high rocky land that is hard to climb
entries.s: We could see snow on top of the _____.

entries.d: Small bright lights you see in the sky at night
entries.s: We looked at the _____ in the sky before going to bed.

entries.d: The round bright thing in the sky at night
entries.s: The _____ was big and bright last night.

entries.d: To make a small room in a tree from wood
entries.s: My dad helped me _____ in the big oak tree.

entries.d: A place where people sleep in tents or cabins
entries.s: We slept in tents at the summer _____.

entries.d: Something you wear over your chest to stay on top of the water
entries.s: You must wear a _____ when you go on the boat.

entries.d: A person who shows you around a new place
entries.s: The _____ told us all about the old castle.

entries.d: A fire you make outside to sit around at night
entries.s: We sat around the _____ outside our tent and told scary stories.

entries.d: A meal you eat outside, sitting on the grass
entries.s: We had a _____ near the river with sandwiches and juice.

entries.d: A small light boat you move with a paddle
entries.s: We got into the small wooden _____ and used a paddle to go down the river.

entries.d: The activity of going in a small boat on a river
entries.s: _____ on the river in a small wooden boat is my favourite activity at summer camp.

entries.d: Water that falls down from a high place over rocks
entries.s: We could hear the loud _____ before we saw the water falling from the high cliff.

entries.d: Going up big rocks or walls using your hands and feet
entries.s: _____ is exciting but you need to be careful.

entries.d: The lowest part of something
entries.s: The boat sank to the _____ of the lake.

entries.d: On the left side

entries.d: The centre part, not the edges
entries.s: There is a big tree in the _____ of the park.

entries.d: On the right side
entries.s: My name is on the _____ page of the list.

entries.d: A warm jacket that keeps the rain away
entries.s: Take your _____ with the hood — it might rain this afternoon and you want to stay dry.

entries.d: A strong hat that keeps your head safe from things falling
entries.s: Workers on a building site must wear a plastic _____ to protect their head from falling bricks.

entries.d: Totally, completely
entries.s: This pizza is _____ delicious! It is the best I have ever eaten.

entries.d: Really, in fact
entries.s: I thought the film was bad, but it was _____ quite good — I was surprised.

entries.d: A place where children do fun outdoor activities

entries.d: To feel scared about something

entries.d: Even if, but still
entries.s: I went outside _____ it was raining.

entries.d: To think something is important and worry about it
entries.s: She _____ about her little brother very much and helps him with homework every day.

entries.d: A trip in a car from one place to another
entries.s: It was a long _____ in the car from Vienna to Salzburg. Dad was behind the wheel for three hours.

entries.d: A big door in a wall or fence that you walk or drive through
entries.s: We opened the _____ and walked into the garden.

entries.g: etw. gut können, gut in etw. sein
entries.d: To do something well
entries.s: My friend is very _____ drawing pictures. Her art always wins prizes.

entries.d: Words that start a fairy tale
entries.s: _____, a girl lived in a little house in the woods.

entries.s: There were many woolly _____ on the green hill. The farmer cuts their wool every spring.

entries.s: The _____ walked across the field with his dog.

entries.d: To believe that someone is good and honest
entries.s: I _____ my best friend with all my secrets.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with TRUST?

entries.d: A short time
entries.s: We waited for a _____ and then the bus came.

entries.d: To clean the dishes after eating
entries.s: Can you help me _____ the dirty plates and forks after dinner?

entries.d: Not dead, still living
entries.s: The little bird fell from the tree but it was still _____.

entries.d: A loud sound you make when you are hurt or scared
entries.s: We heard a loud _____ from the garden — someone was hurt!

entries.d: Words you say when you are leaving
entries.s: See you tomorrow. _____ I need to catch my bus.

entries.d: Words you say to someone who is having a bad time
